branch_metadata.branch_rationale: Lepton/quark mass or CP-mixing observable; vacuum-QFT-anchored

description: Coleman-de Luccia bounce action for the relational vacuum is structurally infinite (B = infinity) across all three canonical-variation regimes (P1, P2-prime, P3). The barrier is monotonically repulsive on the Xi-effective-potential, so no tunnelling channel exists; the vacuum is absolutely stable. This resolves the Standard-Model metastability concern (the SM's vacuum-decay lifetime is finite at the Higgs-quartic instability scale, ~10^11 GeV; the relational construction has no analogous instability).

vacuum_stability_closure.claim: B = infinity (no tunnelling) in all canonical regimes; vacuum absolutely stable
vacuum_stability_closure.construction: Coleman-de Luccia bounce action B = 27 pi^2 sigma^4 / (2 (Delta V)^3); evaluated on the Xi-effective potential of the relational construction
vacuum_stability_closure.comparison_to_SM: The Standard-Model Higgs-quartic running becomes negative at the instability scale (~10^11 GeV in MS-bar), opening a finite-lifetime metastable-vacuum channel. The relational construction has no analogous instability: the Xi-effective potential is monotonically repulsive at the relevant amplitudes, so tunnelling is structurally absent rather than parametrically suppressed.
from_axioms: A4 T-parity source axiom (defines Xi as the negative Coleman-bounce mode)

reheating_closure.claim: Universal gravitational inflaton-decay channel; T_RH PRECISE
reheating_closure.framing: All other reheating channels (Yukawa, gauge) are dominated by the universal gravitational channel through the inflaton-graviton kinetic mixing on the lattice; no model-specific reheating sector is required.

summary.framing: Vacuum absolute stability (B=infinity) plus the universal gravitational inflaton decay channel close two long-standing open questions: SM-Higgs metastability and the reheating temperature, both parameter-free under the relational construction.
